content.claim: Within-school achievement variance is not significantly related to age of first selection across OECD countries; one study found nearly no change in within-school variance between countries tracking at age 10 versus age 15, suggesting between-school stratification, not within-school homogeneity, is the primary mechanism of early tracking
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Single study with limited country sample
Cross-sectional design cannot establish causal direction
Does not account for selection effects in school composition itself
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
